title: FR: More generic vault connector
author: bendem
body_snippet:
I saw that mRemoteNG supports vault connector for a single secret server. Most applications nowadays provide a more generic protocol allowing users to write tooling decoupled from the application, opening a path to integration with any password storage with minimal glue code.


## Expected Behavior

mRemoteNG defines a protocol, either a script must take parameter X and Y and return a json blob of schema Z, or a webserver must respond to GET request X with parameters Y and respond with a pl...

iterations.description: This feature requests a more generic vault connector to integrate with various external credential management systems, enhancing flexibility and security.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): This feature requests a more generic vault connector to integrate with various external credential management systems, enhancing flexibility and security.
Approach: Implement a new ICredentialRepository that abstracts interaction with various vault services, define a corresponding configuration interface, integrate it into the CredentialServiceFactory, and provide a UI for configuring vault connections and mapping credentials to mRemoteNG connection properties.
